note: Entity Share shares content entities between separate Drupal sites over JSON:API. The base entity_share module is a shell (a config landing page + one permission); the load-bearing facts live in its submodules: the SERVER submodule (entity_share_server) defines the `channel` config entity (binds an entity type + bundle + language and exposes it as a JSON:API collection), and the CLIENT submodule (entity_share_client) defines the `remote` config entity (a server's url + auth plugin) plus `import_config`. LIMITATION: a real end-to-end sync needs a SECOND Drupal site running the server — that cannot be exercised on this single site. So the introspection (medium) and execution (hard) cases ground ONLY on creating/reading the `channel` and `remote` CONFIG ENTITIES on this one site; they never perform an actual cross-site pull. Setup/reset ensure the entity_share_server + entity_share_client submodules are enabled so those config entity types exist. All introspection/execution cases touch ONLY eval_intro_* / eval_* ids and leave any real channels/remotes untouched.

evals.prompt: I want to copy content from one Drupal site to another using Entity Share. Which module do I enable on the site that HOLDS the content, and which on the site that RECEIVES it, and what protocol moves the content between them?
evals.expected_output: Enable Entity Share Server (entity_share_server) on the source/content-holding site to define channels, and Entity Share Client (entity_share_client) on the receiving site to define a remote and pull; content moves over core JSON:API. A site can be both.

evals.prompt: In Entity Share on Drupal 11, name the config entity type the SERVER side uses to expose content and the config entity type the CLIENT side uses to point at a server, and say what each one holds.
evals.expected_output: The server defines a `channel` config entity (binds channel_entity_type + channel_bundle + channel_langcode and optional JSON:API filters/sorts, with role/user/permission access) exposing that set as a JSON:API collection; the client defines a `remote` config entity (a server base url + an auth plugin) that it pulls channels from.

evals.prompt: What underlying Drupal transport/API does Entity Share use to share entities between sites, rather than defining its own custom REST endpoints?
evals.expected_output: Entity Share builds on core JSON:API — the server exposes channels as JSON:API collections and the client consumes them, so no custom REST resources are needed.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), an Entity Share server channel with id `eval_intro_channel` already exists. Inspect the running site and tell me which entity type and bundle it exposes.
evals.expected_output: The channel eval_intro_channel exposes the node entity type, article bundle (read live from the `channel` config entity's channel_entity_type=node and channel_bundle=article).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), an Entity Share client remote with id `eval_intro_remote` already exists. Inspect the running site and tell me the URL it points at.
evals.expected_output: The remote eval_intro_remote points at https://hub.eval.example.com (read live from the `remote` config entity's url property).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), the Entity Share server channel `eval_intro_channel` already exists. Inspect the running site and tell me which language (langcode) it is configured to expose.
evals.expected_output: The channel eval_intro_channel exposes English, langcode `en` (read live from the `channel` config entity's channel_langcode property).

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), set up the Entity Share SERVER side to share Article content. Create a `channel` config entity with id `eval_channel` that exposes the node entity type, article bundle (English). Enable the entity_share_server submodule if needed and apply it live; don't just describe it. One-line summary. Note: an actual cross-site pull is out of scope (there is no second site) — only the channel config entity is required.
evals.expected_output: A `channel` config entity eval_channel exists with channel_entity_type=node and channel_bundle=article. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), create an Entity Share SERVER channel that shares Basic page content. Create a `channel` config entity with id `eval_page_channel` exposing the node entity type, page bundle. Enable the entity_share_server submodule if needed and apply it live; don't just describe it. One-line summary. Note: no second site exists, so only the channel config entity is required — no live pull.
evals.expected_output: A `channel` config entity eval_page_channel exists with channel_entity_type=node and channel_bundle=page. Verified against live site state.

evals.prompt: On this Drupal site (drush available), set up the Entity Share CLIENT side. Create a `remote` config entity with id `eval_remote` pointing at the server URL https://source.eval.example.com (Anonymous authorization is fine). Enable the entity_share_client submodule if needed and apply it live; don't just describe it. One-line summary. Note: no reachable server exists, so only the remote config entity is required — no live pull.
evals.expected_output: A `remote` config entity eval_remote exists with url https://source.eval.example.com. Verified against live site state.
